title: erythroxylum jamaicense
intro: Erythroxylum jamaicense is a species of flowering plant in the family Erythroxylaceae, commonly known as the Jamaican coca or coca de Jamaica. The plant's leaves have been traditionally used for their medicinal and stimulant properties, containing alkaloids such as cocaine and cinnamoylcocaine, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The leaves have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, and are also believed to have analgesic and anti-anxiety properties. The leaves can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and are also used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory issues and as a topical treatment for skin conditions.
